name: semiotic
description: React data visualization library for charts, networks, and beyond

function.name: LineChart
function.description: Line traces with curve interpolation, area fill, and point markers. Use for time series, trends, and continuous data.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

xAccessor.description: Key or accessor function for x-axis values

yAccessor.description: Key or accessor function for y-axis values

lineBy.description: Key to group data into separate lines

lineDataAccessor.description: Key for the coordinates array within each line object

showPoints.description: Show data point markers on the line

pointRadius.description: Radius of point markers when showPoints is true

fillArea.description: Fill the area under the line

forecast.description: Forecast overlay config — tagged training/observed/forecast region with optional envelope. See ForecastConfig.

anomaly.description: Anomaly overlay config — ±σ band + anomaly dot annotations. See AnomalyConfig.

band.description: Asymmetric min/max envelope drawn under the line. `{ y0Accessor, y1Accessor, style?, perSeries?, interactive? }` or an array of those for percentile fans. Distinct from `forecast`/`anomaly` (computed) — band is pure data passthrough. Hovered datum is enriched with `band: { y0, y1 }` and `bands: [...]`.

function.name: AreaChart
function.description: Filled area chart with optional stroke line. Use for showing volume or magnitude over time.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

areaBy.description: Key to group data into separate areas

lineDataAccessor.description: Key for the coordinates array within each area object

showLine.description: Show stroke line on top of area

forecast.description: Forecast overlay config — tagged training/observed/forecast region with optional envelope. See ForecastConfig.

anomaly.description: Anomaly overlay config — ±σ band + anomaly dot annotations. See AnomalyConfig.

band.description: Asymmetric min/max envelope drawn under the area. See LineChart.band — same shape, same enrichment.

function.name: StackedAreaChart
function.description: Stacked area chart with optional normalization to 100%. Use for part-to-whole trends over time.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

areaBy.description: Key to group data into stacked areas

function.name: Scatterplot
function.description: Individual data points plotted by x/y position with optional size and color encoding.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

sizeRange.description: Min and max radius for sizeBy scaling

regression.description: Overlay a regression line. true = linear, 'linear' | 'polynomial' | 'loess' = method, or full RegressionConfig object. Sugar over the trend annotation.

forecast.description: Forecast overlay config — tagged future points + optional envelope. See ForecastConfig.

anomaly.description: Anomaly overlay config — ±σ band + anomaly dot annotations. See AnomalyConfig.

function.name: QuadrantChart
function.description: Scatterplot divided into four labeled, colored quadrants by center lines. Use for BCG matrices, priority matrices, and any 2x2 strategic framework.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). xCenter.description: X-coordinate of the vertical center line. Defaults to midpoint of x domain.

yCenter.description: Y-coordinate of the horizontal center line. Defaults to midpoint of y domain.

quadrants.description: Configuration for the four quadrants: { topRight, topLeft, bottomRight, bottomLeft }, each with { label, color, opacity? }

function.name: MultiAxisLineChart
function.description: Dual Y-axis line chart for comparing two series with different scales on the same x axis. Data is unitized (normalized to [0,1]) internally; left axis shows series[0] values and right axis shows series[1] values in original units. Falls back to standard multi-line if not exactly 2 series.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

data.description: Array of data objects shared by both series

series.description: Exactly 2 series configs for dual-axis mode. Each: { yAccessor, label?, color?, format?, extent? }

function.name: CandlestickChart
function.description: OHLC candlestick bars, or a range chart when open/close are omitted. Honors mode (primary/context/sparkline). Range variant degrades cleanly: endpoint dots + wick, sized against canvas height so sparkline rows don't render marble-sized dots.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

highAccessor.description: Required. Upper bound (candlestick high or range top).

lowAccessor.description: Required. Lower bound (candlestick low or range bottom).

openAccessor.description: Optional. Pair with closeAccessor for OHLC; omit both to render a range chart.

regression.description: Overlay a regression line on the bubbles. Same shape as Scatterplot's regression prop.

function.name: ConnectedScatterplot
function.description: Scatterplot where points are connected in order, showing trajectories through 2D space. Viridis-colored start→end, white halo under lines.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). forecast.description: Forecast overlay config — same shape as LineChart's forecast prop.

anomaly.description: Anomaly overlay config — ±σ band + anomaly dot annotations.

function.name: BarChart
function.description: Vertical or horizontal bars for categorical comparisons.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). sort.description: Sort bars: false, true, 'asc', 'desc', or comparator function

regression.description: Overlay a regression line through the bar tops. Accepts true (linear), a method ('linear' | 'polynomial' | 'loess'), or a full RegressionConfig. Pixels resolve through the band scale.

function.name: StackedBarChart
function.description: Stacked bars for part-to-whole comparisons across categories. Requires stackBy to define the stacking dimension.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

stackBy.description: Key to define the stacking dimension (required)

function.name: LikertChart
function.description: Visualize Likert scale survey responses. Horizontal (default): diverging bar chart centered at 0% — negative levels extend left, positive right, neutral (if odd count) split 50/50 across centerline. Vertical: stacked 100% bar chart. Supports raw integer scores (1-based, aggregated automatically) or pre-aggregated (question, level, count) data. The levels array defines polarity: first half = negative, second half = positive, center = neutral (if odd). Works with any scale size (3-point to 7-point+). Supports push API for streaming — accumulates raw data and re-aggregates on each push.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

data.description: Array of raw response or pre-aggregated data objects

levels.description: Ordered response labels, most negative to most positive (required). Odd count = center is neutral.

valueAccessor.description: Integer score field for raw response mode (1-based: score 1 → levels[0])

levelAccessor.description: Level name field for pre-aggregated mode. Each value must match an entry in levels.

function.name: RidgelinePlot
function.description: Overlapping density distributions for comparing distributions across categories. Each category gets a density curve that can overlap with adjacent rows.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

valueAccessor.description: Key for numeric values to build distributions from

bins.description: Number of bins for density estimation

amplitude.description: Unitless multiplier of row height (>1 creates overlap)

function.name: FunnelChart
function.description: Funnel visualization with two orientations. Horizontal (default): steps top-to-bottom with centered bars and trapezoid connectors; multi-category mirrors around center axis. Vertical: steps on x-axis as vertical bars with hatched dropoff stacking (solid = retained, hatched = dropoff from previous step); multi-category renders grouped bars.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

data.description: Array of data objects with step and value fields

valueAccessor.description: Key for numeric value per step

categoryAccessor.description: Key to split each step into mirrored categories (optional)

orientation.description: Horizontal (default): centered bars top-to-bottom with trapezoid connectors. Vertical: vertical bars with hatched dropoff stacking — solid = retained, hatched = dropoff from previous step. Multi-category renders grouped bars in vertical mode.

connectorOpacity.description: Opacity of trapezoid connectors between steps (0-1). Horizontal orientation only.

showCategoryTicks.description: Show category tick labels on ordinal axis

function.name: SwimlaneChart
function.description: Categorical lanes with sequentially stacked items colored by subcategory. Unlike StackedBarChart, the same subcategory can appear multiple times in the same lane — items stack left-to-right (horizontal) or bottom-to-top (vertical) in data order. Supports brush for value-axis selection and push API for streaming.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

data.description: Array of data objects. Omit for push API mode.

subcategoryAccessor.description: Key for item subcategory (color grouping within lanes). Required. Duplicate subcategories in the same lane stack sequentially.

valueAccessor.description: Key for item size/duration along the value axis

orientation.description: Horizontal renders lanes as rows; vertical as columns.

roundedTop.description: Rounded corner radius (px) applied to the outermost ends of each lane — left+right for horizontal, top+bottom for vertical. Middle segments stay square; single-segment lanes round all four corners.

onBrush.description: Callback with { r: [min, max] } or null when brush clears

showCategoryTicks.description: Show lane labels on the category axis

function.description: Overview + detail chart with linked zoom. Wraps an XY chart with a minimap navigation pane.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

function.name: ChoroplethMap
function.description: Geographic choropleth map with colored regions based on data values.

function.name: DistanceCartogram

function.name: ProcessSankey
function.description: Temporal sankey with a real time x-axis. Edges carry startTime/endTime; nodes can declare an explicit xExtent lifetime. Use for timestamped flow events (PR commits, campaign-finance contributions, supply-chain shipments).

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

edges.description: Array of timed edge records with source, target, value, startTime, endTime. Omit for push-mode.

nodes.description: Optional array of node objects. Nodes may carry an `xExtent: [start, end]` to bound the lane explicitly.

axisTicks.description: Optional [{ date, label }] tick array for the time axis.

pairing.description: Edge-side pairing strategy at transit nodes.

packing.description: Lane reuse — pack lifetime-disjoint nodes into the same row.

particleStyle.description: ParticleStyle config — same shape as SankeyDiagram. Defaults from DEFAULT_PARTICLE_STYLE (radius 3, opacity 0.7, spawnRate 0.1, maxPerEdge 50).

function.name: DifferenceChart
function.description: Two-series difference chart: fills the area between series A and series B with a color that switches at each crossover — A's color where A > B, B's color where B > A. Crossovers are linearly interpolated so segments meet at zero-width vertices. Both series can be drawn as overlay lines on top of the fill. Classic uses: temperature anomaly (actual vs. normal), forecast accuracy (actual vs. predicted), budget variance.

annotations.description: Annotation objects to render on the chart. Each must have a `type` field. Position using your data field names (e.g. { type: "widget", month: "Jan", revenue: 500 }). Supported types: "widget" (arbitrary HTML/React content via foreignObject — v3 replacement for htmlAnnotationRules), "label" (callout with connector), "callout" (circle + label), "text" (plain label), "y-threshold" (horizontal reference line), "x-threshold" (vertical reference line), "band" (shaded y-region), "enclose" (circle around points), "rect-enclose" (rect around points), "highlight" (colored dots on filtered points), "trend" (regression line), "envelope" (upper/lower bounds), "anomaly-band" (mean ± stddev), "forecast" (extrapolated trend). Widget annotations accept: content (ReactNode), dx, dy, width, height, anchor ("fixed"|"latest"|"sticky"). Threshold annotations accept: value, label, color, strokeWidth, strokeDasharray. Enclose annotations accept: coordinates (array of data objects), label, color, padding.

axisExtent.description: Tick endpoint mode. "nice" rounds endpoints to readable values; "exact" pins the first and last tick to the actual data min and max with equidistant intermediates. Affects XY x/y axes and ordinal value axis only.

data.description: Array of `{x, a, b}` objects. Omit for push API mode.

seriesALabel.description: Display label for series A in legend + tooltip

seriesAColor.description: Fill color when series A is higher. Defaults to var(--semiotic-danger).

seriesBColor.description: Fill color when series B is higher. Defaults to var(--semiotic-info).

showLines.description: Draw the two series as overlay lines on top of the fill

showPoints.description: Show points at each data vertex on the overlay lines

gradientFill.description: Tip→base gradient across each segment; same shape as AreaChart.gradientFill

xExtent.description: Fixed x domain `[min, max]`. Either bound may be `undefined`.

yExtent.description: Fixed y domain `[min, max]`. Either bound may be `undefined`.

windowSize.description: Max raw rows in the push buffer; older rows evict FIFO. Recommended for long-running streams.
